questions.question: Describe the layered architecture of the Android platform with a neat diagram.
keyPoints: Linux Kernel: hardware drivers, process/memory management, security
HAL: standard interfaces for hardware access
Native Libraries + ART: C/C++ libraries and Android Runtime
Application Framework: Java/Kotlin APIs for app development
Applications: system apps and user-installed apps

questions.question: Compare ART (Android Runtime) with Dalvik Virtual Machine.
keyPoints: ART uses AOT+JIT compilation; Dalvik uses JIT only
ART provides faster app startup and execution
ART has improved garbage collection (concurrent, compacting)
ART replaced Dalvik from Android 5.0 Lollipop

questions.question: Explain the role of the Linux Kernel in Android architecture. Why was Linux chosen as the base?
keyPoints: Provides hardware drivers for display, camera, Bluetooth, etc.
Handles process management, memory management, and power management
Provides security through user-based permissions and process isolation
Linux chosen because it is open source, well-tested, and has proven driver model
Supports shared libraries, multitasking, and multi-threading

questions.question: What is the Hardware Abstraction Layer? Explain how it enables hardware interaction in Android.
keyPoints: HAL provides standard interfaces between framework and hardware
Contains vendor-specific modules for each hardware component
Framework calls HAL interface, HAL calls kernel drivers
Allows hardware vendors to implement drivers without modifying the framework
